RepurpOS

Long COVID / Post-Acute COVID Sequelae (PACVS)

Disease intelligence for Post-Acute COVID/Vaccination Syndrome: 104 alterations and 675 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

US DALYs10.64MWHO GHE 2021 · all ages
Global DALYs243.03MWHO GHE 2021 · worldwide
US mortality424.2K deaths/yrWHO GHE 2021
Global mortality8.72M deaths/yrWHO GHE 2021 · worldwide
NIH funding$98MFY2025 · RCDC estimate
Funding levelSeverely Underfunded
Spontaneous remissionSpontaneous resolution at 12 months: ~50-70% of patients; persistent symptoms at 2 years in 30-50%; severely…
Best-intervention remissionNo approved pharmacotherapy; Paxlovid (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir) — PANORAMIC trial showed no reduction in Long COVID…
Gap sizeVery large — no proven pharmacotherapy; enormous research investment but no approved treatment as of mid-2026
Primary barrierbiological (mechanism not fully understood) + practice (no evidence-based protocol)
Full remission profile →
Home/ RepurpOS/ Long COVID / Post-Acute COVID Sequelae (PACVS)

Disease overview

Alterations
104
Molecular (A)
100
Clinical (B–E)
4
Direct drugs
546
Via biomarker
125
Natural agents
11
Natural products
10
Merged ranked
675
With trial/literature evidence

Sources: Seeds, Open Targets, HPO, ClinicalTrials.gov, DGIdb, ChEMBL, OSMF Chronic Disease Review, PubMed MeSH · Pipeline phase 7 · Updated 2026-07-05

Remission & chronicity

Spontaneous remission
Spontaneous resolution at 12 months: ~50-70% of patients; persistent symptoms at 2 years in 30-50%; severely debilitating long COVID in 10-15% at 1 year without intervention
Best-intervention remission
No approved pharmacotherapy; Paxlovid (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir) — PANORAMIC trial showed no reduction in Long COVID risk at 6 months if started in acute phase; BC007 (autoantibody neutraliser) — Phase 2 signal; low-dose naltrexone — observational data positive; antihistamine combination (famotidine + cetirizine) — anecdotal
Drug-free remission
—
Relapse after remission
—
Chronicity
—
Gap size
Very large — no proven pharmacotherapy; enormous research investment but no approved treatment as of mid-2026
Primary barrier
biological (mechanism not fully understood) + practice (no evidence-based protocol)
Barrier detail: Long COVID is a heterogeneous syndrome with multiple proposed mechanisms (viral persistence, autoimmunity, microclotting, dysautonomia, mitochondrial dysfunction); no single treatment works for all patients; fragmented care with poor coordination; many clinicians still do not acknowledge Long COVID as a diagnosis

Last SoC change: 2024 — NIH RECOVER initiative released clinical trial protocols for LDN, anticoagulation, anti-viral, and SSRIs; no approved treatment exists; WHO case definition 2021; CDC guidance 2022

Remission data layers: disease_db_100 · source-locked manual data · confidence: high

Related: Biomarker atlas · Intervention atlas

Identifiers: MONDO: MONDO:0100096 · EFO: EFO_1002048 · MESH: D000086382 · UMLS_CUI: C5397822

Right to Try Relevance Critical

Eligibility: This disease meets the federal and state right-to-try legal standard (21 U.S.C. § 360bbb-0a and Montana SB 535): it lacks disease-modifying therapy with indication-specific FDA approval. Patients with Post-Acute COVID/Vaccination Syndrome may be eligible to access investigational therapies outside of clinical trials under right-to-try frameworks.
Investigational Agents
538
Phase 3: 210, Phase 2: 270, Phase 1: 58
Most Advanced Phase
Phase 3
Highest dev. stage
Gene Targets
104
Actionable
Pipeline Compounds (Top 6)
Darapladib, Fenretinide, Amubarvimab, Aviptadil, Bamlanivimab, Bemnifosbuvir
Legal Standard: Right-to-try requires the disease lacks "adequate approved therapy for that specific disease" (not symptom-management drugs). This distinction means a disease with antidepressants available still qualifies if no FDA approval exists for THAT disease indication.
View Right-to-Try Compendium

Therapeutics

Direct disease associations, biomarker-linked drugs, OSMF narrative-review natural agents, and merged ranking. Only agents with registry trials or published clinical literature are shown by default.

From opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease — Metabolic modulation as a therapeutic strategy for post-acute vaccination syndrome (PACVS). Candidates for further study, not approved treatments.

DrugTypePhaseEvidence ScoreTrials & literatureSourcesLinks
ANAKINRA Biologic Approved Strong 105
7 Cochrane 11 Meta-analysis 16 Trial pubs 12 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Treatment of multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (World journal of pediatrics : WJP, 2024)
  • Cochrane review A meta analysis on the utility of Anakinra in severe COVID-19 disease (Cytokine, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Clinical efficacy and safety of interleukin-1 blockade in the treatment of patients with COVID-19: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Annals of medicine, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Anakinra for the treatment of COVID-19 patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis (European journal of medical research, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Anakinra was not associated with lower mortality in hospitalised COVID-19 patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Reviews in medical virology, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Assessment of the available therapeutic approaches for severe COVID-19: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Scientific reports, 2023)
  • Systematic review Effectiveness of early Anakinra on cardiac function in children with multisystem inflammatory syndrome of COVID-19: a systematic review (BMC infectious diseases, 2024)
  • Systematic review Safety and efficacy of pharmacological approaches available for multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C): a systematic review (The Turkish journal of pediatrics, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Tocilizumab, sarilumab and anakinra in critically ill patients with COVID-19: a randomised, controlled, open-label, adaptive platform trial (Thorax, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Anakinra efficacy in COVID-19 pneumonia guided by soluble urokinase plasminogen activator receptor: Association with the inflammatory burden of the host (International journal of antimicrobial agents, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Tocilizumab reduces hypercoagulation in COVID-19 - Perspectives from the coagulation and immunomodulation Covid assessment (Coag-ImmCovA) clinical trial (Thrombosis research, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication HEPARIN-BINDING PROTEIN LEVELS PREDICT UNFAVORABLE OUTCOME IN COVID-19 PNEUMONIA: A POST HOC ANALYSIS OF THE SAVE TRIAL (Shock (Augusta, Ga.), 2024)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Digital interventions for substance use disorders in young people: rapid review (Substance abuse treatment, prevention, and policy, 2023)
  • Cochrane review A review of the characteristic properties of selected tobacco chemicals and their associated etiological risks (Reviews on environmental health, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Prevalence of cardiovascular risk factors according to Life's Essential 8 in children and adolescents during the COVID-19 pandemic: A systematic review and meta-analysis including 1 526 173 participants from 42 countries (Pediatric obesity, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking and vaping alter genes related to mechanisms of SARS-CoV-2 susceptibility and severity: a systematic review and meta-analysis (The European respiratory journal, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking prevalence among hospitalized COVID-19 patients and its association with disease severity and mortality: an expanded re-analysis of a recent publication (Harm reduction journal, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Hospitalised Current Smokers and COVID-19 (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2020)
  • Meta-analysis The association of smoking status with SARS-CoV-2 infection, hospitalization and mortality from COVID-19: a living rapid evidence review with Bayesian meta-analyses (version 7) (Addiction (Abingdon, England), 2021)
  • Systematic review The Intertwining of Posttraumatic Stress Symptoms, Alcohol, Tobacco or Nicotine Use, and the COVID-19 Pandemic: A Systematic Review (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2022)
  • Systematic review Transdermal nicotine in non-smokers: A systematic review to design COVID-19 clinical trials (Respiratory medicine and research, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparison of remote to hybrid methods on recruitment and retention rates in a smoking cessation trial (Drug and alcohol dependence, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Adaptive Smoking Cessation Using Precessation Varenicline or Nicotine Patch: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of financial voucher incentives provided with UK stop smoking services on the cessation of smoking in pregnant women (CPIT III): pragmatic, multicentre, single blinded, phase 3, randomised controlled trial (BMJ (Clinical research ed.), 2022)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of regdanvimab in patients with mild to moderate COVID-19: A rapid review and meta-analysis (British journal of clinical pharmacology, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Outpatient Treatment of Confirmed COVID-19: Living, Rapid Practice Points From the American College of Physicians (Version 1) (Annals of internal medicine, 2023)
  • Cochrane review SARS-CoV-2-neutralising monoclonal antibodies for treatment of COVID-19 (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication A randomized, double-blind, Phase 1, single- and multiple-dose placebo-controlled study of the safety and pharmacokinetics of IN-006, an inhaled antibody treatment for COVID-19 in healthy volunteers (EBioMedicine, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Real-world clinical effectiveness of Tixagevimab/Cilgavimab and Regdanvimab monoclonal antibodies for COVID-19 treatment in Omicron variant-dominant period (Frontiers in immunology, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Safety, Virologic Efficacy, and Pharmacokinetics of CT-P59, a Neutralizing Monoclonal Antibody Against SARS-CoV-2 Spike Receptor-Binding Protein: Two Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Phase I Studies in Healthy Individuals and Patients With Mild SARS-CoV-2 Infection (Clinical therapeutics, 2021)

  • Meta-analysis Remdesivir for the treatment of children hospitalized with COVID-19: a systematic review and meta-analysis (BMC pulmonary medicine, 2026)
  • Systematic review Efficacy and safety of remdesivir for patients with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 infection: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials (Indian journal of pharmacology, 2026)
  • Systematic review Exploring molecular interactions of drugs in different biologically active solvents: A comprehensive review for safe and efficient drug delivery systems (International journal of biological macromolecules, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Bayesian reanalysis of early remdesivir for the treatment of COVID-19 in outpatients with high risk of progression to severe disease (PloS one, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Cytomegalovirus DNAemia in Hospitalized Adults With SARS-CoV-2 Infection Requiring Supplemental Oxygen: Virologic and Clinical Characteristics and Association With Outcomes (The Journal of infectious diseases, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Pharmacokinetics of SARS-CoV-2 RNA Polymerase Inhibitor Remdesivir in Participants With Moderate and Severe Hepatic Impairment (Clinical and translational science, 2025)

2 Cochrane 6 Meta-analysis 11 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Digital interventions for substance use disorders in young people: rapid review (Substance abuse treatment, prevention, and policy, 2023)
  • Cochrane review A review of the characteristic properties of selected tobacco chemicals and their associated etiological risks (Reviews on environmental health, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Prevalence of cardiovascular risk factors according to Life's Essential 8 in children and adolescents during the COVID-19 pandemic: A systematic review and meta-analysis including 1 526 173 participants from 42 countries (Pediatric obesity, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking and vaping alter genes related to mechanisms of SARS-CoV-2 susceptibility and severity: a systematic review and meta-analysis (The European respiratory journal, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking prevalence among hospitalized COVID-19 patients and its association with disease severity and mortality: an expanded re-analysis of a recent publication (Harm reduction journal, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Hospitalised Current Smokers and COVID-19 (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2020)
  • Meta-analysis The association of smoking status with SARS-CoV-2 infection, hospitalization and mortality from COVID-19: a living rapid evidence review with Bayesian meta-analyses (version 7) (Addiction (Abingdon, England), 2021)
  • Systematic review The Intertwining of Posttraumatic Stress Symptoms, Alcohol, Tobacco or Nicotine Use, and the COVID-19 Pandemic: A Systematic Review (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2022)
  • Systematic review Transdermal nicotine in non-smokers: A systematic review to design COVID-19 clinical trials (Respiratory medicine and research, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparison of remote to hybrid methods on recruitment and retention rates in a smoking cessation trial (Drug and alcohol dependence, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Adaptive Smoking Cessation Using Precessation Varenicline or Nicotine Patch: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of financial voucher incentives provided with UK stop smoking services on the cessation of smoking in pregnant women (CPIT III): pragmatic, multicentre, single blinded, phase 3, randomised controlled trial (BMJ (Clinical research ed.), 2022)

2 Cochrane 6 Meta-analysis 11 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Digital interventions for substance use disorders in young people: rapid review (Substance abuse treatment, prevention, and policy, 2023)
  • Cochrane review A review of the characteristic properties of selected tobacco chemicals and their associated etiological risks (Reviews on environmental health, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis Prevalence of cardiovascular risk factors according to Life's Essential 8 in children and adolescents during the COVID-19 pandemic: A systematic review and meta-analysis including 1 526 173 participants from 42 countries (Pediatric obesity, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking and vaping alter genes related to mechanisms of SARS-CoV-2 susceptibility and severity: a systematic review and meta-analysis (The European respiratory journal, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Smoking prevalence among hospitalized COVID-19 patients and its association with disease severity and mortality: an expanded re-analysis of a recent publication (Harm reduction journal, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Hospitalised Current Smokers and COVID-19 (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2020)
  • Meta-analysis The association of smoking status with SARS-CoV-2 infection, hospitalization and mortality from COVID-19: a living rapid evidence review with Bayesian meta-analyses (version 7) (Addiction (Abingdon, England), 2021)
  • Systematic review The Intertwining of Posttraumatic Stress Symptoms, Alcohol, Tobacco or Nicotine Use, and the COVID-19 Pandemic: A Systematic Review (International journal of environmental research and public health, 2022)
  • Systematic review Transdermal nicotine in non-smokers: A systematic review to design COVID-19 clinical trials (Respiratory medicine and research, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparison of remote to hybrid methods on recruitment and retention rates in a smoking cessation trial (Drug and alcohol dependence, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Adaptive Smoking Cessation Using Precessation Varenicline or Nicotine Patch: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of financial voucher incentives provided with UK stop smoking services on the cessation of smoking in pregnant women (CPIT III): pragmatic, multicentre, single blinded, phase 3, randomised controlled trial (BMJ (Clinical research ed.), 2022)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Prevalence of Cardiovascular Complications in Coronavirus Disease 2019 adult Patients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Iranian journal of medical sciences, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Cardiac biomarkers, cardiac injury, and comorbidities associated with severe illness and mortality in coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19): A systematic review and meta-analysis (Immunity, inflammation and disease, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Serum biomarkers for prediction of mortality in patients with COVID-19 (Annals of clinical biochemistry, 2022)
  • Cochrane review The common risk factors for progression and mortality in COVID-19 patients: a meta-analysis (Archives of virology, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Update on SARS-CoV-2 infection in children (Paediatrics and international child health, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis A composite ranking of risk factors for COVID-19 time-to-event data from a Turkish cohort (Computational biology and chemistry, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Cardiac biomarkers and COVID-19: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of infection and public health, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis Cystatin C, COVID-19 severity and mortality: a systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of nephrology, 2022)
  • Systematic review Hashimoto's thyroiditis-related myopathy in a patient with SARS-CoV-2 infection: A case report and systematic literature review (Medicine, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Metabolic reprogramming of endothelial-related pathways in COVID-19 patients treated with hyperbaric oxygen therapy: a randomized clinical trial (Scientific reports, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication The effects of 3-month supplementation with synbiotic on patient-reported outcomes, exercise tolerance, and brain and muscle metabolism in adult patients with post-COVID-19 chronic fatigue syndrome (STOP-FATIGUE): a randomized Placebo-controlled clinical trial (European journal of nutrition, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication The effects of inspiratory muscle training on biomarkers of muscle damage in recovered COVID-19 patients after weaning from mechanical ventilation (Chronic respiratory disease, 2024)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Effect of N-Acetylcysteine on mortality in COVID-19 patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Inflammopharmacology, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis COVID-19 clinical outcomes and N-acetylcysteine (CoViNAC study): a GRADE compliant meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials with molecular docking and dynamics simulation studies with Mpro of SARS-CoV-2 (Cellular and molecular biology (Noisy-le-Grand, France), 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Is N -acetylcysteine effective in treating patients with coronavirus disease 2019? A meta-analysis (Journal of the Chinese Medical Association : JCMA, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis N-acetylcysteine efficacy in patients hospitalized with COVID-19 pneumonia: a systematic review and meta-analysis (Romanian journal of internal medicine = Revue roumaine de medecine interne, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Response to Intravenous N-Acetylcysteine Supplementation in Critically Ill Patients with COVID-19 (Nutrients, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Evaluation the efficacy and safety of N-acetylcysteine inhalation spray in controlling the symptoms of patients with COVID-19: An open-label randomized controlled clinical trial (Journal of medical virology, 2023)

Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
